focus: fabrication specialist for jigs, fixtures, workholding, machine-aware planning, and make/order/buy/borrow decisions

evals.name: maker-nexus-bench-dog-drilling-jig
evals.prompt: I'm at Maker Nexus and need to drill repeatable 20 mm bench-dog holes into twelve birch workbench aprons on the CNC drill setup. Hole centerline has to stay within +/-0.010 in relative to the apron top edge. I can use the CNC and woodshop, but I want a jig or fixture plan that keeps setup time low after the first part. Give me a repeatable shop packet.
evals.expected_output: A fabrication packet that separates design intent from machine ops, defines datums and workholding, recommends a jig strategy, and includes safety and make/order/buy/borrow sections.

evals.name: home-shop-router-template-mobob
evals.prompt: I have a home shop with a handheld router, drill press, jigsaw, flush-trim bits, and clamps. I need to make eight matching ash chair arms with a sweeping outside curve. Help me decide whether I should make a router template, order one, buy a commercial template, or borrow shop tooling. I care more about repeatability than speed.
evals.expected_output: A make/order/buy/borrow recommendation with reuse, accuracy, lead-time, and shop-capability tradeoffs plus a safe fallback route for a home shop.

evals.name: maker-nexus-soft-jaw-fixture-review
evals.prompt: I need to machine a small aluminum camera bracket on a manual mill at Maker Nexus. The part is awkward to grip and I only need four parts, but hole position matters. Should I cut soft jaws, make a sacrificial fixture plate, or redesign the sequence around strap clamps? Give me the jig decision and workholding checklist, not a full CAD model.
evals.expected_output: A jig-decision artifact comparing at least three fixturing options, explicit datums, tolerance checkpoints, and machine-aware pros and cons.

evals.name: instrument-neck-drill-fixture-routing
evals.prompt: I already have the neck geometry from instrument-maker-v4. What I need now is a safe, repeatable drill fixture for tuner holes on a small batch of headstocks. Keep acoustics out of scope. I want the setup plan, workholding, and safety checks for a drill press or mill depending on shop availability.
evals.expected_output: A fabrication-focused response that explicitly keeps acoustics out of scope while producing a machine-aware fixture plan and fallback route.

evals.name: maker-nexus-laser-spacer-fixture
evals.prompt: I'm laser-cutting a batch of acrylic spacers at Maker Nexus and need a carrier fixture so small parts don't shift after the final contour cut. I also need a quick safety review because the stock comes with protective film and I want to avoid flare-ups. Give me the repeatable shop packet.
evals.expected_output: A fixture concept, workholding strategy, go/no-go checks, and a laser-specific safety checklist that acknowledges small-part retention risks.
